message:
chore(beads): close br-import-hangs-jsonl-drift with rebuild workaround

Closes franken_networkx-br-import-hangs-jsonl-drift (P1, was BLOCKER)
after applying the documented DB-rebuild workaround from
/home/ubuntu/.claude/skills/fixing-beads-problems. Steps recorded in
the bead's close_reason for future agents:

1. Snapshot via fixing-beads-problems/scripts/snapshot-and-doctor.sh
2. Kill stuck br processes (fuser .beads/beads.db → kill PIDs)
3. tmp=.beads/beads.db.rebuild_$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)
   br sync --import-only --rebuild --json --db "$tmp" \
     --no-auto-import --no-auto-flush
4. br doctor --db $tmp confirms counts.db_vs_jsonl OK,
   sqlite.integrity_check OK, db.write_probe OK
5. mv .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bad_TS
   mv $tmp .beads/beads.db (and the -wal sidecar)

Result: DB now at 1242/1242 records aligned with JSONL.

Root cause is in beads_rust (the br tool) — the import path silently
fails to advance under the lock contention pattern that arises when
sibling agents are issuing concurrent br create/show/list calls. Fix
belongs upstream in beads_rust's sync code path; this bead is closed
to unblock franken_networkx work. The workaround is now well-documented
in case the symptom recurs.

Also picks up the Cargo.lock ftui 0.3.1 → 0.4.0 bump that other agents
in the workspace landed during this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
